title: Kimi K3 disrupting EDA?
author: J
text:
Y

Y.H
Active member

They demonstrated designing a chip in 48 hrs. Is that necessarily a very amazing feat? Or we need to compare the yield , performance and thermals of that design? Basically to test it.

Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 autonomously designed a functional semiconductor chip in 48 hours using exclusively open-source tools — bypassing Cadence Design Systems and Synopsys entirely — and shares of both EDA giants were under pressure Friday, falling roughly 9%, as investors weighed the long-term implications for their software businesses.

Cadence & Synopsys slide as Kimi K3 designs chip in 48h using no proprietary EDA

Cadence (NASDAQ: CDNS) and Synopsys (NASDAQ: SNPS) are the two dominant players in Electronic Design Automation, selling the proprietary toolchains, Cadence’s Genus, Innovus, and Virtuoso; Synopsys’s Design Compiler and IC Compiler, that chipmakers rely on to design, simulate, and verify...

finance.yahoo.com
